subject: Upgrade Junit to 5.6
commit_message:
Upgrade Junit to 5.6

- Update @Before -> @BeforeEach, @After -> @AfterEach
- Update Mockito usage
- Update assertThrows so not to expect exceptions

Issue-ID: SDC-3078
Signed-off-by: Neil Derraugh <neil.derraugh@yoppworks.com>
Change-Id: I0b1056698767c4b060e243bbda799253072b4b55

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

I can't get the display name generator to work the way I want it to as in https://leeturner.me/posts/building-a-camel-case-junit5-displaynamegenerator/.  Spring boot is pulling in Junit 5.3.2 which doesn't have the annotation for DisplayNameX.  If I override it to 5.6.0 then the tests fail to run from maven.  ¯\_(ツ)_/¯  If I upgrade spring boot to 2.3.0.RELEASE then some, ubt not all, unit tests fail with what *looks* like a bug in Apache commons Base64.  If I use java.util.Base64 from that point all the unit tests pass from Maven, but I still haven't implemented the DisplayNameGenerator and I'm way outside the scope of this task.  I'll note this in a Jira task and it can be revisited.  Leaving the long method names as-is until then.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1
> 
> I can't get the display name generator to work the way I want it to as in https://leeturner.me/posts/building-a-camel-case-junit5-displaynamegenerator/.  Spring boot is pulling in Junit 5.3.2 which doesn't have the annotation for DisplayNameX.  If I override it to 5.6.0 then the tests fail to run from maven.  ¯\_(ツ)_/¯  If I upgrade spring boot to 2.3.0.RELEASE then some, ubt not all, unit tests fail with what *looks* like a bug in Apache commons Base64.  If I use java.util.Base64 from that point all the unit tests pass from Maven, but I still haven't implemented the DisplayNameGenerator and I'm way outside the scope of this task.  I'll note this in a Jira task and it can be revisited.  Leaving the long method names as-is until then.

hi Neil,
did you try like this:
git diff pom.xml
diff --git a/pom.xml b/pom.xml
index a64eab7..4aa7c43 100644
--- a/pom.xml
+++ b/pom.xml
@@ -84,7 +84,7 @@
             <dependency>
                 <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                 <artifactId>spring-boot-dependencies</artifactId>
-                <version>2.1.5.RELEASE</version>
+                <version>2.2.7.RELEASE</version>
                 <type>pom</type>
                 <scope>import</scope>
             </dependency>
@@ -106,6 +106,26 @@
             <artifactId>junit-jupiter</artifactId>
             <version>${junit.jupiter.version}</version>
             <scope>test</scope>
+            <exclusions>
+                <exclusion>
+                    <groupId>org.junit.jupiter</groupId>
+                    <artifactId>junit-jupiter-api</artifactId>
+                </exclusion>
+                <exclusion>
+                    <groupId>org.junit.jupiter</groupId>
+                    <artifactId>junit-jupiter-params</artifactId>
+                </exclusion>
+                <exclusion>
+                    <groupId>org.junit.jupiter</groupId>
+                    <artifactId>junit-jupiter-engine</artifactId>
+                </exclusion>
+            </exclusions>
+        </dependency>
+        <dependency>
+            <groupId>org.junit.jupiter</groupId>
+            <artifactId>junit-jupiter-api</artifactId>
+            <version>${junit.jupiter.version}</version>
+            <scope>test</scope>
         </dependency>
         <dependency>
             <groupId>org.mockito</groupId>

All tests are GREEN.

Hi Vasyl,

All tests are green in my IDE with ^^.  However if we run `mvn clean test` we see that none of the tests run in `security-util-lib`.

I will address it in my next task: https://jira.onap.org/browse/SDC-3091.

Thanks!
